components.name: Button

notes: Use fn() for event handlers and hide direct controls for them.
Prefer one or two decision stories over rendering every enum combination.

components.blueprintMarkdown:
# Button blueprint

_Framework_: react

## Props
- `children`: `ReactNode`
- `size`: `'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'`
- `tone`: `'neutral' | 'brand' | 'danger'`
- `disabled`: `boolean`
- `loading`: `boolean`
- `onClick`: `() => void` _(event)_

## Default args
- `children`: "Preview"
- `size`: "sm"
- `tone`: "neutral"

## Controls
- `size`: {"control": "radio", "options": ["sm", "md", "lg"]}
- `tone`: {"control": "inline-radio", "options": ["neutral", "brand", "danger"]}
- `onClick`: {"control": false, "useFn": true}

## Recommended stories
- Default (baseline)
- Small (decision)
- Large (decision)
- Brand (decision)
- Danger (decision)
- Disabled (state)
- Loading (state)
- LongContent (boundary)

## Notes
- Use fn() for event handlers and hide direct controls for them.
- Prefer one or two decision stories over rendering every enum combination.

Hide internal props from docs tables and controls: className.
Prefer one or two decision stories over rendering every enum combination.

components.blueprintMarkdown:
# Alert blueprint

_Framework_: react

## Props
- `title`: `string`
- `message`: `string`
- `tone`: `'info' | 'success' | 'danger'`
- `dismissible`: `boolean`
- `onDismiss`: `() => void` _(event)_
- `className`: `string` _(hidden)_

## Default args
- `title`: "Component title"
- `message`: "Helpful supporting text."
- `tone`: "info"

## Controls
- `tone`: {"control": "inline-radio", "options": ["info", "success", "danger"]}
- `onDismiss`: {"control": false, "useFn": true}
- `className`: {"control": false, "table": {"disable": true}}

## Recommended stories
- Default (baseline)
- Danger (decision)
- Success (decision)
- Dismissible (state)
- LongContent (boundary)

## Notes
- Use fn() for event handlers and hide direct controls for them.
- Hide internal props from docs tables and controls: className.
- Prefer one or two decision stories over rendering every enum combination.

components.name: StatusPill
components.componentSource: {label}
components.storySource:
<script module lang="ts">
  import { defineMeta } from '@storybook/addon-svelte-csf';

  import StatusPill from './StatusPill.svelte';

  const { Story } = defineMeta({
    title: 'Fixtures/Svelte/StatusPill',
    component: StatusPill,
    tags: ['autodocs'],
    args: {
      label: 'Synced',
      tone: 'neutral',
      selected: false,
      compact: false,
    },
    argTypes: {
      tone: {
        control: 'inline-radio',
        options: ['neutral', 'brand', 'danger'],
      },
    },
  });
</script>

<Story name="Default" />

<Story
  name="Brand"
  args={{
    tone: 'brand',
    label: 'Ready',
  }}
/>

<Story
  name="Danger"
  args={{
    tone: 'danger',
    label: 'Failed',
  }}
/>

<Story
  name="Selected"
  args={{
    selected: true,
  }}
/>

<Story
  name="Compact"
  args={{
    compact: true,
  }}
/>

notes: Prefer one or two decision stories over rendering every enum combination.
